project_context.app_type: React Native Expo Mobile App (or Mobile-First PWA if restricted to React.js)

theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 2 (Performance Pro) - Modified for Tactical Security
theme_and_archetype.rationale: High visibility, command-center focus suitable for security operations. High-contrast overlays for job shift cards.

fonts.headings: Barlow Condensed (or generic sans-serif bold if custom fonts aren't loaded)

cards.shift_card: Background #111827, 1px solid border #1F2937. Subtle inner padding. Header with Job Title and Pay Rate right-aligned. Status badges absolute positioned or top row.
cards.shadows: Minimal shadow, rely on 1px solid borders for definition (Tactical Minimalism).

buttons.primary: Background #2C7BFF, text white. Fully rounded corners (rounded-lg). Min height 48px for touch targets.

navigation.bottom_tabs: Background #0A0E1A, top border 1px solid #1F2937. Active icon color #2C7BFF, inactive #6B7280.
navigation.top_header: Background #0A0E1A, displays 'Miami, FL' with location pin icon and notification bell.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text against #0A0E1A and #111827 meets minimum 4.5:1 ratio.
accessibility.touch_targets: All interactive elements must be at least 44x44 points for mobile tap accuracy.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include 'data-testid' (if React DOM) or 'testID' (if React Native) following kebab-case role-based convention (e.g., 'shift-card-view-button').

image_urls.description: Miami skyline night for location headers
Concert stage night crowd for Hard Rock Stadium shifts

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a mobile application. If your environment is set up for React Native Expo, use Expo components (View, Text, ScrollView) and write .js files.

If your environment only supports React.js web, build a mobile-first Web App layout: restrict the main container to max-width (e.g., max-w-md mx-auto), use a fixed bottom navigation bar, and emulate a mobile experience.
ONLY output .js files (not .tsx) as requested by the system constraints.
Implement the 'Performance Pro' archetype: Deep navy backgrounds, bright blue and neon green accents. Use 1px solid borders for card definitions instead of heavy drop shadows.
MUST assign 'data-testid' (web) or 'testID' (React Native) to all interactive elements following kebab-case role-based convention (e.g., testID='login-submit-button', testID='shift-card-1').
The app requires 4 bottom tabs: Browse, My Shifts, Messages, Profile.
Shift cards must display: Job Title, Venue, Location Pin, Time Range, Pay Rate (top right), Status Badges (OPEN/FILLING FAST), Officers needed, and a primary 'View Shift' button.
Profile screen must feature specific verification badges: Background Checked, State Licensed, ID Verified, Insured & Bonded.

DO NOT use raw HTML tags (div, span) if in React Native. DO NOT use Shadcn-web primitives if in React Native. If in Web, you may use Shadcn but apply heavy custom Tailwind classes to override the default white/light theme into our deep navy tactical theme.
Avoid generic 'SaaS blue'. Use the precise HEX codes provided.
